key_insight: A general-purpose ACE interatomic potential can capture niobium’s diverse atomic environments with near-DFT accuracy while remaining efficient enough for large-scale simulation.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable prediction of niobium behavior for defects, phonons, high-pressure response, dislocation motion, and fracture in computational materials design.
method: Trained an atomic cluster expansion (ACE) potential on thousands of DFT-generated niobium structures.
Covered a wide range of local environments to improve transferability and robustness.
Benchmarked against empirical and ML potentials across multiple target properties, including a near-million-atom fracture simulation.
evidence_signal: The model was validated on thousands of DFT structures and tested across phonons, high-pressure behavior, dislocation-glide barriers, and a near-million-atom molecular dynamics fracture case.
watch_item: Transferability still depends on whether new simulations stay within the diverse but finite DFT training domain; extrapolation to unseen defect chemistries or extreme conditions should be checked carefully.
paper_title: A general-purpose atomic cluster expansion interatomic potential for niobium
